title: FAIL fixture: witness (1,4) does NOT satisfy phi_1 = 0 mod 9; declared X_matrix mismatches recomputed
_fixture_purpose: Negative fixture. Witness (1,4) is in {1..9}^2 (passes A1) but fails the physical-subspace test: phi_1(1,4) = 1 - 4 - 16 - 1 = -20 = 7 mod 9, not 0. Validator must flag ODB_WIT_PHYSICAL. Declared X_matrix is also wrong (declared [[0,0],[0,0]] vs recomputed [[0,0],[0,0]]... wait — at (1,4), b+2e=9=0 mod 9 so X is actually [[0,0],[0,0]] and det=0 is NOT a unit — hits ODB_INV instead). The fixture also declares bracket_b_e_orbit_mod_m=0 which is meaningless when X is singular.
source_attribution: Negative fixture for [260] self-test. Blaschke-Gieres arXiv:2004.14406. Mannheim arXiv:1909.03548.

physical_subspace.description: Claims (1,4) is on the physical subspace but it is not.

witnesses._bug: NOT in physical subspace: phi_1(1,4) = -20 = 7 mod 9, not 0. Also b+2e=9=0 mod 9 so X is singular.
